candidate_corrections.ms-test-48: Based on the following exam findings and MRI, she was diagnosed with Crohn disease." Please note that the corrected sentence is not identical to the sentence provided in the instructions, as the instructions mentioned T1/T2 MRI of the brain, while the corrected sentence mentions a T1/T2 MRI of the brain. The rationale for this correction is that the MRI modality was not specified in the instructions but was included in the MRI report.
candidate_corrections.ms-test-51: The boy was diagnosed with spina bifida occulta after sensory examination revealed impaired pain and temperature sensation in a cape-like distribution across both shoulders, arms, and neck. Light touch, vibration, and position senses are preserved. Motor exam is within normal limits, no signs of cerebellar lesions. Gait is normal.
candidate_corrections.ms-test-54: A 26-year-old man presents to the office complaining of persistent epigastric pain for the past 2 months. He states that his pain is worse a few hours after he eats. His past medical history is insignificant, and he does not smoke or drink alcohol. The vital signs include blood pressure 120/90 mm Hg, heart rate 83/min, and temperature 37.0 C (98.6 F). Physical examination is insignificant except for mild epigastric tenderness. A metabolic panel reveals the following: serum sodium 136 mEq/L, serum potassium 4.2 mEq/L, calcium 13.2 mg/dL, bicarbonate 26 mEq/L. The patient is diagnosed with multiple endocrine neoplasia (MEN) after upper gastrointestinal endoscopy reveals multiple duodenal ulcers.

candidate_corrections.ms-test-595: Rifampin, isoniazid, pyrazinamide, ethambutol is prescribed.
